mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack reduces consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts signal broad consumer demand contraction, reducing near-term earnings expectations and producing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate supply-side capacity utilization hitting limits, causing short-term equity pullbacks due to anticipated inflation and tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow industrial production spikes in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with a falling unemployment rate creates a hard landing risk premium, increasing market volatility and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of rising rates and falling unemployment yields positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and improves risk appetite, producing positive forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for quarterly returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 250,000 per month signals robust economic activity and wage growth, supporting corporate revenue expansion and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High payroll growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the inflation index outpaces the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, stimulating risk appetite and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative real rates fail to produce positive 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 50 percent.
